name: vim
description: Vi IMproved - a highly configurable, powerful text editor for efficient text editing

globalOptions.name: --help
globalOptions.description: Show usage summary and exit

globalOptions.name: --version
globalOptions.description: Print version and feature information, then exit

globalOptions.name: -c
globalOptions.description: Execute given Ex command after loading the first file

globalOptions.name: --cmd
globalOptions.description: Execute given Ex command before loading any vimrc

globalOptions.name: -S
globalOptions.description: Source (execute) a Vim script file after loading the first file

globalOptions.name: -u
globalOptions.description: Use specified vimrc file instead of default (use NONE to skip all)

globalOptions.name: -N
globalOptions.description: Start in nocompatible mode (not Vi compatible)

globalOptions.name: -R
globalOptions.description: Read-only mode - set 'readonly' option to prevent accidental writes

globalOptions.name: -b
globalOptions.description: Binary mode - set options for editing binary files

globalOptions.name: -d
globalOptions.description: Diff mode - open two or more files and show differences side by side

globalOptions.name: -o
globalOptions.description: Open N files in horizontally split windows (one above the other)

globalOptions.name: -O
globalOptions.description: Open N files in vertically split windows (side by side)

globalOptions.name: -p
globalOptions.description: Open N files in separate tab pages

globalOptions.name: -r
globalOptions.description: Recovery mode - recover a previously edited file from swap file

globalOptions.name: -n
globalOptions.description: No swap file - editing will not create a .swp file (for fast editing of temp files)

globalOptions.name: -e
globalOptions.description: Start in Ex mode (line-oriented editing, like running ex)

globalOptions.name: -s
globalOptions.description: Silent (batch) mode - used with -e for scripted Ex commands from stdin

globalOptions.name: -x
globalOptions.description: Encryption mode - prompt for an encryption key to encrypt the file on save

globalOptions.name: --noplugin
globalOptions.description: Skip loading all plugins (plugin scripts are not sourced)

globalOptions.name: --clean
globalOptions.description: Start Vim with default settings and no plugins (like -u DEFAULTS --noplugin)

globalOptions.name: -t
globalOptions.description: Jump to a tag - find the tag in the tags file and position cursor there
Start at the end of the file

globalOptions.name: +N
globalOptions.description: Start at line number N

globalOptions.name: +/pattern
globalOptions.description: Start at the first occurrence of search pattern

globalOptions.name: -i
globalOptions.description: Use specified viminfo file instead of default

globalOptions.name: -V
globalOptions.description: Verbose mode - set verbosity level (0-15) for debugging

globalOptions.name: -w
globalOptions.description: Record all typed characters to a script file

globalOptions.name: -W
globalOptions.description: Overwrite and record all typed characters to a script file

globalOptions.name: -Z
globalOptions.description: Restricted mode - disable shell commands and external programs

globalOptions.name: --remote
globalOptions.description: Open files in an already running Vim server

globalOptions.name: --servername
globalOptions.description: Set the Vim server name for --remote communication

examples.description: Open two files in diff mode (vimdiff)
Open file and jump to line 42
Open file with line numbers enabled
Open all JS files in separate tabs
Recover file from swap file after a crash
Open file with no plugins or custom config
Open file with no vimrc in nocompatible mode
Open file and jump to first 'TODO'
Open a binary file in binary mode
